surface: Android instrumentation with real SSH fixture on emulator-5554

assertions.reason: Targeted proof used SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest#imported_key_identity_survives_relaunch_and_reaches_real_terminal_session. The saved imported-key identity survived relaunch and the focused legacy PEM probe reached TCP, SSH auth, and shell-channel open against 10.0.2.2:3122, but the shipped app-shell connect path still ended with UI state FAILED and status "Connection timed out while reaching 10.0.2.2:3122." Because the normal relaunched host/session flow did not reach a live terminal session, this assertion fails.

Validated through SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest#encrypted_imported_key_saved_host_stays_blocked_until_repair_then_reuses_same_records. Before repair, the saved host remained linked to the imported-key identity but showed repair-needed/passphrase-required messaging, the connect button stayed disabled, no trust prompt appeared, the session state stayed disconnected with an empty transcript, and known-host trust remained empty. The same blocked behavior was reasserted after clearing the passphrase, satisfying the requirement that no trust/auth/session/fallback transition starts while the identity is non-ready.

Validated through the same targeted method SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest#encrypted_imported_key_saved_host_stays_blocked_until_repair_then_reuses_same_records. The test repaired the passphrase on the existing imported-key identity, verified the same identityId and hostId were preserved, confirmed the host became ready again, and then reconnected on the saved host path to 10.0.2.2:3122 without recreating records. The method finished green in the targeted run, so the repair flow truthfully restores the existing saved host/session path.

commandsRun: env PATH=/root/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:$PATH adb -s emulator-5554 get-state && env PATH=/root/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:$PATH adb -s emulator-5554 shell ls -l /data/local/tmp/aterm-fixture-client_key_legacy_pem /data/local/tmp/aterm-fixture-client_key.pub

frictions.description: The current instrumentation harness produced JUnit XML, HTML, and logcat artifacts but no standalone per-step screenshots for these assertions.
frictions.resolution: Used the targeted connectedDebugAndroidTest artifacts plus the explicit assertion logic in SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest as the evidence set.

frictions.description: The imported-key relaunch test is diagnostic: it passes even when the shipped UI path times out, so VAL-CROSS-001 required manual interpretation from logcat rather than trusting the green test result alone.
frictions.resolution: Mapped the assertion to the logcat lines showing probe success (tcp/auth/shell) and the separate UI timeout failure, then marked the contract status as fail.

summary: Ran one targeted :app:connectedDebugAndroidTest proof on emulator-5554 after confirming the real fixture files /data/local/tmp/aterm-fixture-client_key_legacy_pem and .pub were present. Result: VAL-CROSS-001 failed because the relaunched shipped UI path timed out despite the legacy PEM probe succeeding through TCP/auth/shell; VAL-CROSS-002 and VAL-CROSS-003 passed because blocked imported-key hosts stayed hard-blocked until repair and the repaired flow reused the same saved host/identity records.
